summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened his message by reflecting on the spiritual principle of sowing and reaping from Galatians 6:7-8, using the imagery of his own garden. He challenged the congregation to examine whether they are sowing seeds to please their flesh or to please the Spirit. The core of the sermon was an in-depth exploration of Jesus's Parable of the Sower from Luke 8, which Pastor Hearn titled 'There's a Snake in the Soil.' He explained that the seed is God's Word and the soil is the human heart, warning that the enemy actively works to corrupt that soil while we are spiritually unaware. Pastor Hearn then walked through the four types of hearts Jesus described: the hard heart, which becomes callous through dryness, damage, desensitization, deception, or division; the shallow heart, which lacks deep roots and falls away when temptation comes; the crowded heart, which is choked out by the weeds of worldly attractions, wealth, success, and pleasures; and finally, the fruitful heart, which accepts God's message and produces a great harvest. He emphasized that God desires to give us a new, tender, and responsive heart, as promised in Ezekiel 36:26. The message concluded with a call to wake up from spiritual sleep, to pull out the weeds in our lives, and to fully surrender our hearts to God for renewal and purpose.
mainPoints: We reap what we sow; our spiritual harvest depends on whether we sow to please our flesh or to please God's Spirit.
Our heart is the soil for God's Word, and we must guard against it becoming hard, shallow, or crowded by the enemy's schemes.
God promises to give us a new, pure, and responsive heart when we fully surrender to Him, leading to a fruitful life.

versesMentioned.text: Do not be deceived: God cannot be mocked. A man reaps what he sows.
Whoever sows to please their flesh, from the flesh will reap destruction; whoever sows to please the Spirit, from the Spirit will reap eternal life.
So I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh.

The acts of the flesh are obvious: sexual immorality, impurity and debauchery; idolatry and witchcraft; hatred, discord, jealousy, fits of rage, selfish ambition, dissensions, factions and envy; drunkenness, orgies, and the like. I warn you, as I did before, that those who live like this will not inherit the kingdom of God.
But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, forbearance, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness and self-control. Against such things there is no law.
Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.

A farmer went out to sow his seed. As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path; it was trampled on, and the birds ate it up. Some fell on rocky ground, and when it came up, the plants withered because they had no moisture. Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up with it and choked the plants. Still other seed fell on good soil. It came up and yielded a crop, a hundred times more than was sown." When he said this, he called out, "Whoever has ears to hear, let them hear."

This is the meaning of the parable: The seed is the word of God. Those along the path are the ones who hear, and then the devil comes and takes away the word from their hearts, so that they may not believe and be saved. Those on the rocky ground are the ones who receive the word with joy when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while, but in the time of testing they fall away. The seed that fell among thorns stands for those who hear, but as they go on their way they are choked by life's worries, riches and pleasures, and they do not mature. But the seed on good soil stands for those with a noble and good heart, who hear the word, retain it, and by persevering produce a crop.
But while everyone was sleeping, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and went away.
Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.
Test me, LORD, and try me, examine my heart and my mind.
Some people are like seed along the path, where the word is sown. As soon as they hear it, Satan comes and takes away the word that was sown in them.

For this people's heart has become calloused; they hardly hear with their ears, and they have closed their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts and turn, and I would heal them.'
rooted and built up in him, strengthened in the faith as you were taught, and overflowing with thankfulness.
Still others, like seed sown among thorns, hear the word; but the worries of this life, the deceitfulness of wealth and the desires for other things come in and choke the word, making it unfruitful.

For you were once darkness, but now you are light in the Lord. Live as children of light (for the fruit of the light consists in all goodness, righteousness and truth) and find out what pleases the Lord.
This is why it is said: "Wake up, sleeper, rise from the dead, and Christ will shine on you."
Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.
I will give you a new heart and put a new spirit in you; I will remove from you your heart of stone and give you a heart of flesh.

Monday.scripture: Galatians 6:7-8 (NIV) - "Do not be deceived: God cannot be mocked. A man reaps what he sows. Whoever sows to please their flesh, from the flesh will reap destruction; whoever sows to please the Spirit, from the Spirit will reap eternal life."
Monday.action: Identify one decision today where you can consciously choose to please God's Spirit instead of your own fleshly desire.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to be aware of my choices today. Give me the strength to sow seeds that please Your Spirit and lead to life.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 4:23 (NIV) - "Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it."
Tuesday.action: Take 5 minutes to assess what you are allowing into your heart through media, conversations, or thoughts. Make one adjustment to guard it better.
Tuesday.prayer: God, show me the areas where I have been careless with my heart. Help me to guard it diligently, for it is the wellspring of my life.

Wednesday.scripture: Luke 8:11, 15 (NIV) - "This is the meaning of the parable: The seed is the word of God... But the seed on good soil stands for those with a noble and good heart, who hear the word, retain it, and by persevering produce a crop."
Wednesday.action: Spend extra time in one passage of Scripture today. Read it slowly and ask God how you can 'retain' or hold onto its truth throughout your day.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I want my heart to be good soil for Your Word. Help me to hear it, hold onto it, and let it produce lasting fruit in my life.

Thursday.scripture: Ephesians 5:14 (NIV) - "Wake up, sleeper, rise from the dead, and Christ will shine on you."
Thursday.action: Identify one area of your spiritual life where you have been 'asleep' or complacent. Take one practical step to re-engage (e.g., restart a prayer time, join a group).
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, wake me up from any spiritual slumber. Shine Your light on the areas I've neglected and give me the desire to rise and follow You with fresh passion.

Friday.scripture: Matthew 13:25 (NIV) - "But while everyone was sleeping, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and went away."
Friday.action: Ask God to reveal one 'weed' in your life—a habit, relationship, or thought pattern that is choking your spiritual growth. Commit to taking the first step to remove it.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, expose any weed the enemy has sown in my life. Give me the courage and strength to pull it out by the roots so I can grow freely for You.

Saturday.scripture: Ezekiel 36:26 (NIV) - "I will give you a new heart and put a new spirit in you; I will remove from you your heart of stone and give you a heart of flesh."
Saturday.action: In a moment of quiet reflection, consciously surrender any hardness, hurt, or sin in your heart to God. Ask Him to create in you a pure and responsive heart.
Saturday.prayer: God, I surrender my heart to You. Please remove any stony, stubborn parts. Create in me a pure heart, and renew a right spirit within me. Have Your way in me.
